payload.text:
Create a complete Factory Production Management System.
Follow real manufacturing flow strictly.
Do NOT simplify logic.
Do NOT assume missing rules.
Everything must follow below structure exactly.

====================================================
1. LEFT SIDE MENU (STRICT ORDER)
====================================================

1. Dashboard
2. Master Data
3. Raw Material
4. Pre Expanding
5. Silo Management
6. Production
7. Finished Goods
8. Furnace Oil Consumption
9. Efficiency
10. Reports

All menu items must align properly.

====================================================
2. DASHBOARD
====================================================

Display:

A. Raw Material Stock Summary
- Show each material separately
- Display in:
  • Kg
  • Bags (auto conversion)

B. Yesterday Production
- Based on DRY WEIGHT (Finished Goods only)

C. Yesterday Fuel Ratio
- Fuel Used ÷ Finished Goods Dry Weight

D. SILO GRAPHICAL DISPLAY

TOTAL SILOS = 13
MAX CAPACITY PER SILO = 500 kg

Display 13 silo graphical images in grid layout.

Each silo must show:
- Silo Number (1–13)
- Current Stock (kg)
- Capacity % (Stock / 500kg)
- Material Name
- Color indicator:
  • Green = Normal
  • Yellow = 70–89%
  • Red = 90%+
  • Grey = Empty

Silo classification:
- Silo 5 → Production Ready
- Silo 10 → Intermediate
- All others → Normal

No pie charts.
No production trend charts.

====================================================
3. MASTER DATA
====================================================

Maintain:
- Finished Goods Items
- Raw Materials
- Production Machines
- Operators

Pre Expander machines must NOT be editable here.

====================================================
4. RAW MATERIAL MODULE
====================================================

A. Receiving
- Item-wise entry
- Stored in kg
- Display kg + bags
- Update stock immediately

B. Issue
- Issue item-wise
- Moves to Pre Expanding stock
- Prevent negative stock

No dry weight logic here.

All entries must have:
- Edit
- Delete
- Audit log

====================================================
5. PRE EXPANDING
====================================================

Machine dropdown:
- Pre Expander 1
- Pre Expander 2

Fields required:
- Date
- Shift
- Raw Material Selection (Dropdown)
- Operator Selection
- Quantity (kg)
- Output Silo (1–13 except 10 if restricted)

Show separately:
Available Issued Stock (item-wise kg)

Validation:
- Cannot exceed available issued stock
- Cannot exceed 500kg silo capacity

Process:
- Deduct from issued stock
- Add to selected silo

No dry weight conversion.

Edit/Delete required.

====================================================
6. SILO MANAGEMENT
====================================================

TOTAL SILOS = 13
MAX CAPACITY = 500 kg each

----------------------------------------------------
6A. ONE-TIME OPENING STOCK (SILO)
----------------------------------------------------

Button:
"Set Opening Silo Stock"

- Enter silo-wise opening stock
- Allowed ONLY once
- After saving → disabled permanently

----------------------------------------------------
6B. MANUAL SILO STOCK ADJUSTMENT
----------------------------------------------------

Button:
"Adjust Silo Stock"

Fields:
- Silo Number
- Adjustment (+ / -)
- Reason
- User
- Date

Rules:
- Cannot exceed 500kg
- Cannot go below zero
- Log every change

----------------------------------------------------
6C. SECOND EXPANDING PROCESS
----------------------------------------------------

Source:
- Fixed as Silo 10

Fields:
- Date
- Shift
- Operator
- Quantity (kg)

Destination Silo (OUT):
- Dropdown:
  • Silo 5
  • Silo 7

Rules:
- Deduct from Silo 10
- Add to selected silo
- Check 500kg capacity
- Cannot exceed stock

----------------------------------------------------
6D. LARGE EXPANDING BEADS OPTION
----------------------------------------------------

Checkbox:
☐ Mark as Large Expanding Beads

If checked:
- Measured only in kg
- Not finished goods
- No dry weight
- Included in weight efficiency & fuel ratio
- Not included in piece efficiency

All entries editable & deletable.

====================================================
7. PRODUCTION MODULE
====================================================

Two types:
A. Finished Goods (Pieces)
B. Large Expanding Beads (Kg)

----------------------------------------------------
FIELDS REQUIRED
----------------------------------------------------

- Date
- Shift
- Machine
- Operator
- Silo Selection (Not Silo 10)
- Item
- Good Quantity
- Damaged Quantity
- Average Wet Weight

----------------------------------------------------
DRY WEIGHT RULE
----------------------------------------------------

Dry Weight = Average Wet Weight × 94%

Apply ONLY to finished goods.

----------------------------------------------------
CALCULATIONS
----------------------------------------------------

Production Quantity = Good + Damaged

Total Production Weight =
Production Quantity × Dry Weight

Damaged Weight =
Damaged Quantity × Dry Weight

All calculations must use dry weight only for finished goods.

Edit/Delete required.

====================================================
8. FINISHED GOODS MODULE
====================================================

----------------------------------------------------
8A. ONE-TIME OPENING STOCK
----------------------------------------------------

Button:
"Set Opening Finished Goods Stock"

- Item-wise entry
- Allowed only once
- Disabled after saving

----------------------------------------------------
8B. MANUAL STOCK ADJUSTMENT
----------------------------------------------------

Button:
"Adjust Finished Goods Stock"

Fields:
- Item
- Quantity (+/-)
- Reason
- User
- Date

Rules:
- Cannot go negative
- Must log all changes

----------------------------------------------------
8C. STOCK DISPLAY
----------------------------------------------------

Show:
- Item-wise stock
- Total pieces
- Total weight

Auto update from production.

====================================================
9. FURNACE OIL CONSUMPTION
====================================================

Fields:
- Date
- Shift
- Opening Stock (Liters)
- Purchased (Liters)
- Closing Stock (Liters)

Fuel Used =
Opening + Purchased - Closing

Fuel Ratio =
Fuel Used ÷ Total Production Weight

Target:
1.1 L/kg

Edit/Delete required.

====================================================
10. EFFICIENCY WINDOW
====================================================

Display:

1. Piece Efficiency
2. Weight Efficiency
3. Fuel Ratio

Each must show:
- Formula
- Actual numbers used
- Final result %

Graphical Display Required:
- Bar graph for Piece Efficiency
- Bar graph for Weight Efficiency
- Line or bar graph for Fuel Ratio

Clean professional look.

====================================================
11. REPORTS
====================================================

Production Report must include:

1. Date
2. Item
3. UOM
4. Production Quantity
5. Damaged Quantity
6. Good Quantity
7. Shift
8. Average Wet Weight
9. Dry Weight
10. Total Production Weight
11. Total Damaged Weight
12. Machine
13. Operator
14. Fuel Used
15. Fuel Ratio

Filters:
- Date range
- Shift
- Item
- Machine

Export to Excel.

====================================================
12. DATA CONTROL (GLOBAL RULE)
====================================================

ALL modules must include:

- Edit option
- Delete option
- Audit log:
   • Old Value
   • New Value
   • User
   • Date
   • Time

- Prevent negative stock
- Prevent exceeding silo capacity
- Permanent database storage
- No data reset after restart

====================================================
STRICT PROCESS FLOW
====================================================

Raw Material →
Issue →
Pre Expanding →
Silo 10 →
Second Expanding →
Silo 5 or 7 →
Production →
Finished Goods →
Efficiency →
Reports

Dry weight used ONLY for finished goods.
Opening stock allowed only once.
Manual adjustments always logged.
All stock movements traceable.

**Defining Project Scope**

Okay, I'm currently focused on defining the project's overall scope. I'm taking the user's initial request for a Factory Production Management System and breaking it down into specific modules. My current plan is to use React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS as requested, and I'm mapping out the critical data flows from raw materials to silo management, ensuring clarity on each stage.


**Detailing Data Flow**

I've been meticulously detailing the data flow, specifically from Silo 10's Second Expanding to the Production and Finished Goods stages. I've also integrated the dry weight rule (`Dry Weight = Wet Weight * 0.94`) for Finished Goods calculations. Moreover, I've outlined the strict sidebar order and the silo grid within the dashboard. I'm focusing on the integration of specific UI requirements for each module.


**Outlining the System Structure**

I'm now outlining the system's core structure. I plan to use `useStorage` for local storage persistence as requested. Global state will cover inventory, silos, and logs. The sidebar will be fixed, and the dashboard is planned as a 13-column (or responsive) grid for silos, with color-coded logic. The file structure is planned, including types, constants, the app entry point, and key components and pages.

payload.text:
**Troubleshooting TypeScript Errors**

I'm currently investigating TypeScript errors in `pages/FinishedGoods.tsx`. Specifically, line 25 reports that 'pcs' and 'weight' are unknown properties on a type, currently defined as 'unknown'. I need to determine the correct type for `openingValues` to resolve these errors, likely a specific interface or type definition that includes these properties.


**Refining Type Inference**

I've realized the core issue stems from how TypeScript infers types when using `Object.values` on `openingValues`, even though I defined it as `Record<string, { pcs: number; weight: number }>`. It seems the inference doesn't always work as expected, leading to `any[]` or `unknown[]` and type errors on `pcs` and `weight`. I suspect the environment's `Object.values` type definition might be the culprit. I'm now exploring how to explicitly cast or refine this to ensure the correct type is used.


**Confirming Type Safety**

I'm now certain `Object.values` is returning an `unknown[]`, despite `openingValues` being declared as `Record<string, { pcs: number; weight: number }>`. The error message confirms this, pinpointing the source of the problem. I've re-examined the environment and TS configuration, and can confirm that casting or a more explicit iteration approach is needed. The core issue is the type inference for `Object.values`, which needs refinement. Line 25 specifically highlights the need to ensure that the array elements are of the expected object type, and that `v` is correctly typed as `{pcs: number, weight: number}`.

entries.description: Fixed TypeScript errors where Object.values was returning unknown types, preventing access to 'pcs' and 'weight' properties. Added explicit type casting for opening stock values.

Do NOT redesign the full system.
Only correct fuel ratio logic and chart display.

====================================================
1. FUEL RATIO CALCULATION CORRECTION
====================================================

Current calculation is WRONG.

Correct Formula:

Fuel Ratio = Total Fuel Consumption (Liters) / Total Production Weight (Kg)

Where:

Total Production Weight =
   (Finished Goods Weight in Kg)
 + (Large Beads Weight in Kg)

Important:
• Do NOT use total pieces
• Do NOT use dry weight twice
• Use final calculated production weight only
• If Total Production Weight = 0 → show "0" and prevent division error

Example:

Fuel Consumption = 500 Liters
Total Production Weight = 25,000 Kg

Fuel Ratio = 500 / 25,000 = 0.02 L/kg

Display Result As:
0.02 L per Kg

====================================================
2. FUEL RATIO PIE CHART
====================================================

Add a separate pie chart for Fuel Ratio.

Chart Title:
"Fuel Consumption Ratio"

Pie Chart Must Show:

• Fuel Used Portion
• Remaining Production Efficiency Portion

Calculation for Pie Percentage:

Step 1:
Define a standard benchmark value (Example: 0.025 L/kg)

Step 2:
Fuel Efficiency % =
(Standard Ratio / Actual Ratio) × 100

If Actual Ratio <= Standard Ratio:
   Show as Efficient

If Actual Ratio > Standard Ratio:
   Show as Over Consumption

Pie Chart Sections:

• Efficient Portion (%)
• Excess Fuel Portion (%)

Requirements:

• Full visible pie (no clipping)
• Percentage visible inside chart
• Clear legend
• Responsive size
• Center aligned

====================================================
3. DISPLAY BELOW PIE CHART
====================================================

Below chart show:

• Total Fuel Used (Liters)
• Total Production Weight (Kg)
• Fuel Ratio (L/kg)
• Standard Benchmark Used
• Efficiency %

### 🔥 CRITICAL FIX – DELIVERY WINDOW NOT RENDERING

PROBLEM:
Delivery tab opens but window is completely empty.

CAUSE:
Delivery component is not rendering layout container.
Possibly:
- return null
- wrong conditional rendering
- missing default state
- tab content not mounted

-----------------------------------------------------

1. FORCE DELIVERY TAB RENDER

Delivery tab must ALWAYS render main container.

Replace Delivery window return structure with:

Render:
-----------------------------------------------------

<DeliveryWindow>
    <Title>Delivery Entry</Title>

    <FormSection>
        Date Picker (required)
        Item Dropdown (required)
        Quantity Input (required)
        Record Delivery Button
    </FormSection>

    <Divider />

    <DeliveryTableSection>
        Table Header:
            Date | Item | Qty | Unit | Edit | Delete
        Table Body:
            Map deliveryEntries
    </DeliveryTableSection>
</DeliveryWindow>

-----------------------------------------------------

2. REMOVE ANY BLOCKING CONDITION

Delete any code like:

IF (!dataLoaded) return null
IF (!items.length) return null
IF (!stockLoaded) return null

Instead use:

IF no data:
    show empty table message:
    "No delivery records yet"

-----------------------------------------------------

3. ENSURE TAB SWITCHING WORKS

In main system tab controller:

IF activeTab === "Delivery"
    Render <DeliveryWindow />

Make sure:
- Tab name matches EXACTLY
- No spelling mismatch
- No case mismatch

Example:
"Delivery" ≠ "delivery"

-----------------------------------------------------

4. INITIAL STATE FIX

On component load:

Initialize:

deliveryEntries = []
selectedDate = today
selectedItem = ""
quantity = ""

Never leave undefined values.

-----------------------------------------------------

5. DEBUG SAFETY CHECK

At top of Delivery window add:

Console log:
"Delivery window mounted"

If not printed → tab not mounted.
If printed → render issue inside layout.

-----------------------------------------------------

6. GUARANTEE UI VISIBILITY

Main container must include:

- Padding
- Background
- Minimum height

Example rule:

Container min-height = 400px

Prevents blank appearance.

-----------------------------------------------------

END OF CRITICAL DELIVERY RENDER FIX
